name: Gold Fields Limited
reporting_currency: USD (US dollars); JSE-listed, ZAR share price & dividends
registered_address: 150 Helen Road, Sandown, Sandton, 2196, South Africa
operating_assets: South Deep (South Africa) — sole SA mine, ~90.2% owned, long-life mechanised
Tarkwa (Ghana) — large open-pit; mining leases expire April 2027 (renewal not automatic)
Damang (Ghana) — handed back to the State 18 April 2026
St Ives, Agnew, Granny Smith, Gruyere (Australia) — Gruyere 100% since Gold Road buy-out Oct 2025
Cerro Corona (Peru) — moving to stockpile/tailings processing; LOM to ~2031
Salares Norte (Chile) — commercial production Aug 2025; ultra-low-cost growth engine
Windfall (Canada) — ex-Osisko Mining (acq. Oct 2024); pre-build, FID guided mid-2026, first gold ~H1 2029

key_people.chairman: John MacKenzie (from May 2026; succeeded Yunus Suleman)

notes: USD reporter, Dec year-end. FY2025 record: revenue US$8,751m (+68%), adjusted EBITDA US$5,637m, attributable profit US$3,567m (incl. ~US$808m one-off Gruyere gain), normalised profit US$2,684m, AISC US$1,645/oz, attributable production 2.438Moz, adjusted FCF US$2,970m (+391%), net debt 0.26x EBITDA. FY2026 guidance: 2.40-2.60Moz, AISC US$1,800-2,000/oz, capex US$1.9-2.1bn. Statutory identifiers (ISIN/registration/LEI) left null pending verification. Identify Gold Fields ≠ Gold Road Resources (acquired) and ≠ GoldFields the US property firm.

Gold_Fields_GFI_12-18m_Outlook_Hormuz_Sc._provenance: Internal IC briefing: 12-18m outlook, management risks, management incentives, and Strait-of-Hormuz Scenarios A (open) vs B (closed). Built from GF FY2025 results, Q1 2026 update, and macro research (gold/Brent/Hormuz).

Gold_Fields_GFI_Scenario_Model_Jun2026.x._provenance: Formula-driven companion scenario model (gold price / AISC / FX -> Revenue, EBITDA, FCF) under Hormuz-open vs Hormuz-closed; calibrated to FY2025 actuals (98.3%).
